destinations.evidence: is_executed uses the primary bitmap for 0-255 and adjusted overflow bucket/bit arithmetic for indices >=256.
destinations.snippet: if index < 256 { ... } else { let adjusted_index = index - 256; let bucket = adjusted_index / 256; let bit_position = adjusted_index % 256; let slot = executed_bitmap_overflow_slot(call_id, bucket); ... }

destinations.evidence: Regression test covers overflow bucket boundaries and primary non-pollution.
Overflow slot derivation is keyed by callId and adjusted bucket.

equivalence_argument: Exact mapping: primary indices use raw bit positions; overflow indices subtract 256 before bucket and bit-position calculation.
rationale: The cluster contains a single high-confidence candidate backed by an end-to-end test of boundary indices around the primary and overflow bitmap split. Neighbor candidates describe scheduler state tracking and transaction plumbing but do not directly refine or supersede this verification-specific bitmap invariant.
statement: For scheduled execution verification, is_executed must evaluate indices 0 through 255 against the primary bitmap, and indices greater than or equal to 256 against overflow bitmaps using adjusted_index = index - 256, bucket = adjusted_index / 256, and bit_position = adjusted_index % 256, without letting overflow bits mark primary indices executed.
suggested_falsification_tests: Seed overflow bucket 0 bits 0, 1, and 255 plus overflow bucket 1 bit 0, then assert is_executed is true for indices 256, 257, 511, and 512 and false for 258 and 513.
Leave the primary bitmap unset while seeding overflow bits, then assert is_executed remains false for primary indices 0 and 255.
Seed primary bitmap bits independently and assert they do not affect overflow indices 256 and above.
summary: Executed bitmap overflow indices use adjusted buckets in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Existing overflow bitmap test would fail if adjusted_index subtraction, bucket, bit position, or primary/overflow split regressed.
title: Executed bitmap overflow indices use adjusted buckets
